message:
Generify Kyber files + functions over security parameters (#4087)

This commit does not change any functionality, except for the feature
probe. It mostly refactors and reformats. The only behavioral change is
that the libcrypto Kyber support feature prope now also checks for Kyber
768 + 1024.
author: Will Childs-Klein

message:
Reinstate Kyber KEM check (#3905)

Some AWS-LC versions with API v20 (such as v1.3.0) do not have the new
KEM API present, so this test would erroneously fail against slightly
older versions of AWS-LC. Now that [AWS-LC has incremented its API][1]
version, we can reliably key off of the new version to ensure that the
KEM API really is present.

For non-AWS-LC or older AWS-LC without an API version, we don't compile
the check because we only expect the Kyber KEM API to be present in
newer AWS-LC builds.

This commit resolves [Issue #3902][2].

[1]: https://github.com/aws/aws-lc/pull/900
[2]: https://github.com/aws/s2n-tls/issues/3902
author: Will Childs-Klein

message:
Migrate Kyber 512 to EVP KEM API (#3853)

New usage is based on [the KEM API design document][1] and [header][2].
Now that we're on a stable KEM API, we remove the
S2N_AWSLC_KYBER_UNSTABLE build flag and always use the linked
libcrypto's Kyber implementation if available. This flag wasn't
previously specified in any of our CI scripts, meaning that
AWS-LC-backed kyber was previously uncovered in s2n's CI. This commit
ensures that coverage and updates the PQ KEM unit test to asserts that
if (non-FIPS) AWS-LC is used as the backing libcrypto, it has the new
Kyber 512 KEM API available.

[1]: https://github.com/aws/aws-lc/blob/main/crypto/kem/README.md
[2]: https://github.com/aws/aws-lc/blob/92c56fbc15f9bb43c4ff062c6c02f7991fd417f6/include/openssl/evp.h#L880

Check for other pre-processor symbol from AWS-LC
author: Will Childs-Klein

message:
Add support for AWS-LC PQ KEM (#3634)

S2N contains an implementation of the Kyber key encapsulation mechanism (KEM) which is made redundant by an equivalent implementation in AWS-LC. We introduce `crypto/s2n_kyber_evp.{c/h}` which implement the KEM functions using AWS-LC's EVP interface. We also introduce a forward compatibility layer which will prefer the AWS-LC implementation if it's available at compile time. Once this changeset is deployed to all known consumers, we can remove the redundant Kyber implementation within S2N.
author: Gerardo Ravago

message:
Add entropy provider callbacks (#2242)

* Adds two entropy provider callbacks;
removes the S2N_DANGEROUS_AES_256_CTR_NO_DF_NO_PR DRBG mode

Most of the changes in this commit were originally written by
colmmacc@ with the commit message:

This change adds new callbacks that allow the s2n caller to
provide the underlying entropy used by s2n's random number
generators.

The caller needs to provide two sources. One source is used
when s2n's RNGs are seeded, or reseeded (this happens every
time the RNG has generated 2^35 bytes if output).

The other source is called every time the RNG is invoked and
is "mixed" in to provide prediction resistance.

This mixing is done in a way where no single entropy source
has control over the final output. Even if one of the entropy
sources or broken or malicious, as long as the other is not, the
final output is still secure.
